data.content:
The build generates `GeneratedInfo.cs` successfully but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile Include>` item in the target so the generated file is passed to the compiler.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file into the intermediate output directory, but the file is never added to the `Compile` item group. The target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`, which means it runs before compilation, but the generated file isn't included in the compilation.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item inside the target after the file is generated, so the compiler knows about it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>"}

<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the intermediate output directory but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild generated the file before compilation, but the compiler had no knowledge of it.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated source file is included in compilation.
